RAZA

Leveraged ETF Signal Research

What is this?

A research workbench for evaluating macro signals as predictors of leveraged ETF returns. 54 instruments (40 leveraged/inverse ETFs + 14 underlying indices), daily data from 2008, and live FRED integration for macro signals.

Workflow

  1. Universe — Browse all ETFs. Click rows to select tickers for charts. Sort any column. Filter by category.
  2. Performance / Drawdowns / Decay — Visualize selected tickers. Growth of $1, underwater chart, and leverage decay (actual vs theoretical) over any time range.
  3. Signals — Enter your FRED API key (free at api.stlouisfed.org). Check boxes to load macro series — VIX, yield curve, credit spreads, etc. Overlay any signal against any ETF. Correlation heatmap updates with your selection.
  4. Regression — Run OLS: pick a dependent variable (any ETF, 1/5/22/63-day forward return), one or more FRED signals as regressors, choose a transform (raw, daily change, z-score, percentile rank) and lag. Get full output: coefficients, t-stats, p-values, R², scatter plot, residuals.
  5. Regime — Pick a signal and threshold(s) to split history into regimes (e.g. VIX <15 / 15-25 / >25). See per-regime return stats, a timeline bar, and conditional distributions for any ETF.
  6. PCA — Select 2+ loaded FRED signals. Get a scree plot (variance explained), loading matrix (which signals drive each PC), and principal component time series.

Quick start

  1. Run python3 serve.py and open http://localhost:8787. The server proxies FRED API requests (FRED doesn't allow direct browser calls).
  2. Go to Signals, paste your FRED API key, click Save & Verify.
  3. Check VIXCLS and T10Y2Y to load them.
  4. Go to Regression. Set Y = TQQQ / 5d fwd, select VIXCLS, transform = Z-Score, click Run.
  5. Go to Regime. Signal = VIXCLS, click Auto for thresholds, ETF = TQQQ, click Analyze.

Math

All statistical computations run client-side in JavaScript. The source is in stats.js with full formula documentation:

  • OLS: β = (X'X)-1X'y — Wooldridge Ch.3
  • PCA: power iteration + deflation — Bishop §12.1
  • t p-values: regularized incomplete beta — Numerical Recipes §6.4
  • Matrix inverse: Gauss-Jordan w/ partial pivoting

Cumulative ratio of actual leveraged return to theoretical (leverage x underlying). Below 1.0 = volatility decay.
